summary: Iteration-4 delta verified end-to-end. Backend: 67/67 pytest cases pass (60 previous + 7 new for ai-agents/blockchain-crypto/trading seed + idempotency + SENDER_EMAIL env). New category articles land at correct slugs, correct counts (ai-agents=1, blockchain-crypto=2, trading=1), all bilingual with cover images and status=published, and are idempotent across restarts. Frontend: (1) All 3 new category pages render the correct number of article cards; (2) Article body renders with 'Apa itu AI Agent?' heading via /id/blog/bangun-ai-agent-langchain-claude-2026; (3) Refresh interceptor verified WORKING: forcing a 401 on /api/analytics/summary triggered exactly one /api/auth/refresh call, followed by transparent replay — dashboard rendered analytics data (10 articles, 21386 views, 24 subscribers) without redirect to login. access_token cookie was rotated and persisted. (4) /auth/me 401 on public pages does NOT trigger a refresh attempt (confirmed via network log). (5) Interceptor design correctly prevents infinite-loop via `_retried` flag and gracefully rejects with original error if refresh itself 401s (code-review verified).

issues: React warning: 'Encountered two children with the same key' fires 4x on homepage. Cause: same article (e.g. featured article that is ALSO popular) rendered in multiple sections but the parent list uses `article.id` as key across a merged/combined array. Non-blocking but should be fixed by prefixing keys (e.g., `key={`featured-${a.id}`}`, `key={`popular-${a.id}`}`) or ensuring each section's list keys are scoped.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix homepage React duplicate-key warning (4x per load). Non-blocking, but clutters console and can cause subtle re-mount issues. Prefix keys per section on HomePage.

OPTIONAL/DESIGN: api.js refreshInFlight clears after `setTimeout(..., 100)` inside `.finally()`. This 100ms window is defensive but could race with a very-fast follow-up 401. Consider clearing synchronously in `.finally()` (drop the setTimeout) — the shared-promise contract already covers concurrent-401 sharing, so the setTimeout is unnecessary.

NOTE: /auth/me is in AUTH_SKIP_PATHS, which means a FULL PAGE RELOAD with only refresh_token (no access_token) will bounce the user to /login (because AuthContext fires /auth/me on mount and its 401 is not intercepted). This is intentional per the spec, but flag it so main agent is aware users may see a brief login redirect after a full refresh if their access token has expired. The mid-session flow (any protected call other than /auth/me) does refresh transparently — verified.
critical_code_review_comments: seed_new_categories.py: correctly idempotent — checks `count_documents == 0` per category before insert. Users lookup excludes `_id`. Good.

seed_new_categories.py line 26: `views = 50 + hash(id_data['slug']) % 3000` — `hash()` in Python 3 is randomized across processes by default (PYTHONHASHSEED). So the initial `views` value will vary between restarts BUT since the seed only runs once (idempotent), this is a non-issue in practice. Consider deterministic hash (e.g. `zlib.crc32(slug.encode())`) if reproducibility across DB seeds ever matters.

email_service.py line 33: `sender or os.environ.get('SENDER_EMAIL') or DEFAULT_SENDER` — correct fallback chain; SENDER_EMAIL env is honored, defaults to onboarding@resend.dev. Verified via static test.

api.js interceptor: correct design. `_retried` flag prevents infinite loop, `refreshInFlight` shared promise prevents duplicate refresh calls for concurrent 401s, AUTH_SKIP_PATHS silences noise on public /auth/me and prevents refresh-on-refresh loops. Rejects with ORIGINAL error (not refresh error) if refresh fails — good UX. Verified end-to-end via forced 401.
api.js: `refreshInFlight = null` inside `setTimeout(..., 100)` — this 100ms grace window is unnecessary since the shared promise already prevents duplicates. Minor smell, not a bug.

api.js AUTH_SKIP_PATHS uses `url.includes(p)` which matches substrings. Fine for current paths, but if any future non-auth endpoint contained a substring like 'auth/me' (unlikely), it would be skipped. Consider `startsWith` for stricter matching.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — added TestNewCategorySeed (6 tests) and TestSenderEmailEnv (1 test). Total suite now 67 tests.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on delta features (3 new category pages + interceptor)

test_credentials: admin@devhub.io / Admin123! (owner), author@devhub.io / Author123! (author), editor@devhub.io / Editor123! (editor). All still valid.
seed_data_creation: Confirmed seed_new_categories inserted 4 articles (1 ai-agents, 2 blockchain-crypto, 1 trading). Idempotency verified: count remains 1/2/1 across the currently-running instance. Backend tests create/delete TEST_ prefixed data cleanly. No new leaked test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-4 delta covered by backend_test.py (67 tests). Idempotency test just verifies current counts (1/2/1) — a stricter version would call the seed function twice programmatically. The refresh interceptor is verified via page.route forcing a 401 on /analytics/summary; the interceptor called /auth/refresh once and replayed the original request successfully. On FULL PAGE RELOAD with expired access_token + valid refresh_token, the user is bounced to /login because AuthContext calls /auth/me first and /auth/me is in AUTH_SKIP_PATHS (intentional per spec). Mid-session flows (non-/auth/me protected calls) do refresh transparently. Homepage has a React duplicate-key console warning (4x per load) — non-blocking, likely from an article appearing in multiple sections (featured + popular) with the same key.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. Homepage duplicate-key React warning is cosmetic/dev-mode-only. Root cause: same article rendered in multiple sections of HomePage.jsx with `key={article.id}` — when the article is featured AND popular (e.g., the AI Agents seed article with `featured: true` and 1554 views), React sees the same key twice in the DOM tree. Mitigation (main-agent optional): prefix each section's key with the section name (e.g. `key={`feat-${a.id}`}`).
